title: Introducing Quarters Theory (I) — Quarters Drawer Script
subtitle: Price Range Subdivision into Hierarchical Quarter Levels with Overshoot Zones
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: EXTRACT — hierarchical price level framework (major → large quarter → small quarter → overshoot), level calculation formulas, and overshoot zone concept. Useful for SL placement beyond key levels and TP targeting.

core_concept.name: Quarters Theory (Ilian Yotov)
core_concept.description: Divide the price range around current price into hierarchical segments: major whole numbers, subdivided into large quarters (÷4), then small quarters (÷10÷4). Each level acts as S/R. Overshoot zones around large quarter levels capture 'liquidity beyond the level' before reversal.
core_concept.key_insight: Markets respect round numbers psychologically. Quarters Theory formalizes this: price levels at MajorStep boundaries (e.g., 1.2000, 1.3000), then quarters (1.2250, 1.2500, 1.2750), then small segments. The overshoot zone captures the common pattern of price briefly exceeding a level before reversing.
core_concept.relevance_to_creviaderiv: Quarter levels give a second layer of S/R beyond our supply/demand zones. Placing SL just beyond a quarter level (outside the overshoot zone) reduces stop hunts. TP targets at large quarter levels are structurally motivated.

major_levels.formula: lowerMajor = floor(price / MajorStep) * MajorStep; upperMajor = lowerMajor + MajorStep

large_quarters.levels: for i in 1..3: level = lowerMajor + i * LQIncrement

small_quarters.levels: for seg in 0..9: for j in 1..3: level = lowerMajor + seg*segmentStep + j*smallQuarterSize
small_quarters.total_levels: 9 segments × 3 inner levels = 27 small quarter levels within one major range
small_quarters.example_eurusd: MajorStep=0.1000 → segmentStep=0.0100, smallQuarterSize=0.0025. First segment: [1.2025, 1.2050, 1.2075], next: [1.2125, 1.2150, 1.2175], etc.

overshoot_zones.zones: for each largeQuarterLevel: zone = [level - overshootOffset, level + overshootOffset]
overshoot_zones.meaning: Price entering this zone may be sweeping liquidity before reversing back through the level

MajorStep.description: Distance between major whole-number levels (e.g., 0.1000 for EUR/USD, 1.00 for Gold, 100 for indices)

DrawOvershootAreas.description: Draw overshoot/undershoot bands around large quarter levels

steps.name: identify_major_range
steps.description: Floor and ceiling of major range from current price and MajorStep

steps.name: compute_large_quarters
steps.description: 3 intermediate lines at MajorStep/4 intervals within the major range

steps.name: compute_small_quarters
steps.description: 27 small quarter lines at MajorStep/10/4 intervals (10 segments × 3 internal)

steps.name: compute_overshoot_zones
steps.description: Band of MajorStep/40 around each large quarter line

steps.name: draw_lines
steps.description: Draw all levels as horizontal lines on chart with configured colors and styles

components_to_build.name: QuarterLevelCalculator
components_to_build.returns: QuarterLevels(major=[lower, upper], large=[3 levels], small=[27 levels], overshoot_bands=[(low, high) x 3])
components_to_build.note: Pure calculation. Instrument-agnostic when MajorStep is calibrated per instrument.

keep: Three-tier hierarchy: major → large quarter → small quarter (clear structural S/R map)
MajorStep as instrument-configurable parameter (works across all instruments)
Overshoot zone formula: MajorStep/40 — standard width that scales with the instrument
floor() formula for finding enclosing major range — simple and exact
Level count: 1 major + 3 large + 27 small = 31 levels within one MajorStep range
discard: Script-only (runs once) — our version must compute dynamically per tick for zone confluence
MQL5 chart drawing APIs — replaced by data output for our ZoneIdentifier
No signal generation — levels are only drawn, not acted on
improve: Auto-calibrate MajorStep: if instrument_type in synthetics → use ATR×10 as MajorStep (adaptive)
Combine quarter levels with our zone boundaries: if zone.upper aligns with large quarter → zone quality +15
Use large quarter levels as TP targets: TP1 = next large quarter, TP2 = major level
Use overshoot zone for SL placement: SL just beyond overshoot band = beyond liquidity sweep area
Multi-major: compute levels for current AND adjacent major ranges (price approaching upper major boundary)

creviaderiv_insights.sl_placement_upgrade: Place SL just outside the overshoot zone of the nearest large quarter level. If demand zone is at 1.2480 and nearest large quarter is 1.2500 with overshoot band [1.2475, 1.2525], SL should be at 1.2470 — beyond the sweep zone. This prevents SL from being caught by routine quarter-level wicks.
creviaderiv_insights.tp_targeting: TP levels at large quarter levels are psychologically reinforced by the market. Instead of ATR-based TP, use nearest large quarter level above entry for TP1, next large quarter for TP2, major level for TP3/P4.
creviaderiv_insights.zone_quality_alignment: If our supply/demand zone boundary aligns within 10 pips of a large quarter level, that zone has institutional reinforcement. Add alignment bonus to zone quality score.
creviaderiv_insights.major_step_for_synthetics: Crash 300: large round numbers are 9000, 9250, 9500, 9750, 10000 (MajorStep=1000). Volatility 75: round levels at every 1000. MajorStep must be calibrated — not one-size-fits-all.
